visualizerParams.title: Counting Summations
visualizerParams.description: Calculate how many different ways a number can be written as a sum of at least two positive integers.
visualizerParams.ghostCode:
def solution(n: int = 100) -> int:
    """
    Returns the number of ways n can be written as a sum of at least two positive integers.
    This is the partition function p(n) minus 1 (to exclude the number itself as a 'sum').
    """
    # Use dynamic programming to find number of partitions
    # ways[i] stores the number of ways to form sum i
    ways = [0] * (n + 1)
    ways[0] = 1 # Base case: 1 way to form 0
    
    # We want to use numbers from 1 to n-1
    for i in range(1, n):
        for j in range(i, n + 1):
            ways[j] += ways[j - i]
            
    return ways[n]


if __name__ == "__main__":
    target = int(input("Enter number: ").strip())
    print(f"Ways to write {target} as a sum: {solution(target)}")
visualizerParams.finalCode:
def solution(n: int = 100) -> int:
    """
    Returns the number of ways n can be written as a sum of at least two positive integers.
    This is the partition function p(n) minus 1 (to exclude the number itself as a 'sum').
    """
    # Use dynamic programming to find number of partitions
    # ways[i] stores the number of ways to form sum i
    ways = [0] * (n + 1)
    ways[0] = 1 # Base case: 1 way to form 0
    
    # We want to use numbers from 1 to n-1
    for i in range(1, n):
        for j in range(i, n + 1):
            ways[j] += ways[j - i]
            
    return ways[n]


if __name__ == "__main__":
    target = int(input("Enter number: ").strip())
    print(f"Ways to write {target} as a sum: {solution(target)}")
